name: Tawhid (Divine Unity)
description: The fundamental doctrine of Islamic monotheism - the oneness of God - elaborated in Sufi metaphysics as the Unity of Being (Wahdat al-Wujud).

name: Wahdat al-Wujud (Unity of Being)
description: The ontological doctrine that all existence is one reality - the Divine Essence manifesting through all forms.

name: Wilaya (Sainthood)
description: The station of spiritual authority and saintly proximity to God - the completion of prophecy.

name: Maqamat (Stations)
description: The fixed stations of spiritual progression - states of being attained through divine grace and human effort.

name: Ahwal (States)
description: The transient states of spiritual experience - divine gifts that descend upon the heart.

name: Nafs (Soul)
description: The psyche or soul - the locus of spiritual transformation - divided into commanding, reproving, and tranquil.

name: Dhikr (Remembrance)
description: The practice of repetitive divine remembrance - the central Sufi devotional method.

name: Sama (Spiritual Concert)
description: The mystical concert - spiritual music and poetry inducing ecstatic states - particularly associated with the Mevleviyya.

name: Adab (Spiritual Etiquette)
description: The refined manners and sacred protocols governing Sufi conduct and relationships.

name: Silsila (Chain of Transmission)
description: The spiritual lineage connecting master to disciple - the chain of authorization.

name: Bayah (Initiation)
description: The oath of allegiance - the contractual commitment between master and disciple.

name: Tariqa (Path)
description: The spiritual method or order - the institutionalized path of practice.
